content: Phase II trial design typically uses randomized controlled methodology, often with placebo or active comparator. Multiple dose arms (e.g., low, medium, high) are evaluated to establish dose-response relationships and select the optimal Phase III dose. Primary endpoints are disease-specific efficacy measures (e.g., HbA1c reduction in diabetes, tumor response in oncology, symptom scores in psychiatry). Statistical power is moderate (often 80% power to detect clinically meaningful effect). Adaptive designs allowing dose modifications or early stopping for futility are increasingly used. Biomarker substudies help identify responsive patient subgroups.
